transcript:
Sarah: Alright everyone, let's get started. We have a lot to cover today. The goal of this sync is to finalize the launch date, resolve the open design questions, and lock in the go-to-market messaging. James, do you want to kick us off with the engineering status?

James: Sure. So the good news is we shipped the last two major backend features this morning — the bulk export and the webhook retry logic. Both are deployed to staging and looking solid. The bad news is that the mobile app still has a crash bug on Android 12 devices specifically. We found it in QA yesterday. It's not blocking the web launch, but if we want to ship mobile at the same time, we need at least another week.

Sarah: Okay, so that's a real problem. We've been advertising the mobile app in our pre-launch emails. Priya, how many signups mentioned mobile specifically?

Priya: Based on the pre-registration survey, about 34% of our waitlist indicated they primarily use mobile. So it's significant. I don't think we can just quietly not launch mobile.

Derek: From a customer success angle, I'd rather delay the whole launch by a week than ship a broken mobile experience. We'll get absolutely hammered in app store reviews and that's hard to recover from.

Sarah: I hear you Derek. James, is one week realistic, or is that optimistic?

James: One week is doable if we pull Raj off the analytics dashboard work and put him on the Android fix full time. The root cause is a memory leak in the video player component. It's isolated, not a systemic issue.

Sarah: Okay. Let's do it. We're moving the launch date to May 28th. James, please reassign Raj to the Android bug starting today. I'll send an update to the waitlist explaining we're taking an extra week to polish the mobile experience — we'll frame it positively.

Priya: That actually works in our favor messaging-wise. I can write a 'we listened to our beta users' angle. Can you get me the specific improvements list, James? I want to be specific in the email.

James: Yeah, I'll put together a changelog summary by Thursday EOD.

Aisha: I wanted to flag something on the design side. The onboarding flow illustrations — we have final assets for web, but the mobile onboarding screens still use placeholder images. If we're launching mobile on the 28th, I need the copy locked by this Friday so I can finish the screens over the weekend.

Sarah: Priya, can you get Aisha the final onboarding copy by Friday?

Priya: Yes, I'll prioritize that. I'll have a draft to Aisha by Thursday morning so she has time to flag anything.

Aisha: Perfect, thank you.

Derek: One thing I want to raise — our support team has not been trained on the new features yet. We had a session scheduled for last week but it got cancelled. If we launch on the 28th, I need to run training by May 23rd at the latest so the team has time to practice before go-live.

Sarah: That's a blocker. Can you set that up, Derek?

Derek: I'll own it. I need the final feature documentation from James to run the training. James, when can I have that?

James: I can have docs ready by May 20th. Is that enough time?

Derek: Tight but workable. Let's say May 20th is the hard deadline for docs.

Sarah: Okay, let's capture all of this. To summarize — launch is May 28th, Raj moves to Android today, James sends changelog to Priya by Thursday, Priya gets copy to Aisha by Thursday morning, James delivers support docs to Derek by May 20th, and Derek runs support training by May 23rd. Did I miss anything?

Priya: One more thing — the paid media campaign was set to go live on May 21st to build up pre-launch momentum. If we're still launching on the 28th, that timeline still works. But I do want to flag that our ad budget for the 7-day window is $18,000, and if the launch slips again, we'd need to pause the campaign and we'd lose some of that spend.

Sarah: Noted. That's a financial risk if we slip again. Everyone needs to hit their deadlines. James, I'm counting on you on the Android fix.

James: We'll get it done.

Sarah: Great. I'll send out a recap email after this call. Talk to you all Thursday for our next check-in. Thanks everyone.
